metadata.adoption_note: Revision of the initial-authored normative LaTeX for whitepaper section 11. v2 consumes both critic siblings of v1 (review BLOCK 38/44 zero flags; audit CLEAN). Changes are prose/structure/LaTeX-mechanical (drop-in-input fixes, RFC-2119 discipline, stable register row IDs, base-layer CT rewording) plus one advisory audit finding declined as a tooling limitation; no normative live/target claim was weakened.
code_ref_resolved: ../../bridge/**/*.rs (35 files; scalar glob per BRIEF resolves the bridge Rust service)
cluster-tax/src/simulation/bridge_import_sweep.rs (import-factor mechanism lives here, not on the release path)
contracts/ethereum/contracts/WrappedBTH.sol (auditor consults manually per BRIEF note; M-1 anvil#718/#724)

constants_marked.name: bridge_threshold_floor
constants_marked.verified_against: ADR 0002; release/bth.rs threshold_floor param; symbolic (no numeric t pinned in code)

constants_marked.name: import_epoch_blocks
constants_marked.verified_against: ADR 0007 (K = 17,280 = 1 day @ 5s); bridge_import_sweep.rs:627 RECOMMENDED_EPOCH_BLOCKS=17280 (simulation only; target-state)

constants_marked.name: import_factor_floor
constants_marked.verified_against: ADR 0007 (F = 1.5x); bridge_import_sweep.rs:624 RECOMMENDED_FLOOR=1500 FACTOR_SCALE (simulation only; target-state)

figure_plan.caption: Wrap / lock -> mint flow (BTH -> wBTH): factor-1 deposit locked, SCP-final, t-of-n attested, minted against orderId; non-factor-1 rejected.
figure_plan.source: inline mermaid: sequenceDiagram user -> reserve (lock factor-1 BTH) -> BTH watcher (SCP final) -> federation (t-of-n attest) -> destination contract (bridgeMint orderId); side branch non-factor-1 -> reject

figure_plan.caption: Unwrap -> import-tagging flow (wBTH -> BTH): burn confirmed, attested, reserve BTH released to fresh stealth address tagged 100% to c_import(m) at import_factor(m) >= F. Target-state; live releases factor-1 (register row IMP-2).
figure_plan.source: inline mermaid: sequenceDiagram user (burn wBTH) -> ETH/SOL watcher (confirm) -> federation (t-of-n release attest) -> reserve (release to fresh stealth, tag c_import(m) at import_factor>=F); annotate target-state vs live factor-1

figure_plan.caption: Federation custody / attestation topology: each validator holds Ed25519 (BTH release + Solana mint) + secp256k1 (Ethereum); threshold envelopes aggregated t-of-n; Ethereum three-Safe split (minter/admin/pauser).
figure_plan.source: inline mermaid: flowchart validators (Ed25519 + secp256k1) -> attestation set (t-of-n) -> {BTH reserve release, Solana SPL/Squads mint, Ethereum minterSafe MINTER_ROLE}; show adminSafe + pauserSafe as separate role holders

rendered.shows: wrap/lock -> mint sequence: factor-1 BTH locked in reserve, SCP finality, t-of-n federation attestation, bridgeMint(to,amount,orderId) on Ethereum/Solana; alt-branch non-factor-1 deposit rejected before any mint

unwrap -> import-tagging sequence: burn wBTH, confirm (depth window / Finalized), t-of-n release attestation, reserve release to fresh stealth address; target-state note tags 100% to c_import(m) at import_factor(m) >= F, live note cites register row IMP-2 (releases factor-1 output)

federation custody/attestation topology: SCP validators (Ed25519 + secp256k1) -> aggregated t-of-n attestation set -> BTH reserve release (Ed25519), Solana SPL/Squads mint (Ed25519), Ethereum minterSafe MINTER_ROLE (secp256k1); Ethereum three-Safe split (minterSafe/adminSafe/pauserSafe)

figures.pdf_note: Body is a whitepaper section (\input into botho-whitepaper.tex); no standalone section PDF is the deliverable. Section-level compile check PASSED: wrapped with whitepaper/preamble.tex + \graphicspath at version dir, xelatex two-pass exit 0, 9pp, all three exhibits/figN PNGs resolved (no missing-image warnings); only expected cross-section \ref warnings (sec:transactions/consensus/monetary/economics live in sibling sections).
figures.mmdc_status: degraded-then-recovered: default mmdc launch failed (pinned puppeteer Chromium absent); system-Chrome executablePath workaround via puppeteer-config.json succeeded for all 3 renders (--scale 3, anvil mermaid-theme.json)

major_advisory: M-1 scalar code_ref glob (anvil#718/#724) — declined, tooling limitation

changes: Removed 5x undefined \addlinespace from register table (whitepaper preamble has no booktabs); verified drop-in \input against real preamble (clean compile, 0 undefined control sequences).
Added stable register row IDs IMP-1..IMP-6; corrected fig2 caption IMP-1 -> IMP-2; wired demurrage-settlement -> IMP-3, mainnet-gate -> IMP-6, divergence note -> IMP-2.
Added RFC-2119 conformance-keywords clause + uppercased MUST/SHALL/SHOULD/MAY at normative points throughout.
Reworded base-layer confidential-amounts claim to cross-reference base-layer CT target (ADR 0006 / sections / botho#902) as target-state, explicitly out of register scope; live = public amounts.
Declined M-1 (tooling limitation, not a spec defect; auditor manually verified all counterparty-contract + simulation claims matched).
Preserved all six register rows and the import-tagging target-state framing verbatim; deferred figure rendering to spec-figures (figure_plan carried forward).
